name: Recharts
what_it_does: Composable charting library for React built on D3 and SVG. Recharts uses a declarative component API — charts are built by composing React components (LineChart, XAxis, YAxis, Tooltip, Legend, etc.). Supports line, bar, area, pie, radar, scatter, and composed charts. Responsive containers handle automatic resizing. The most popular React charting library, used in thousands of dashboards and data applications.
use_cases: Build data dashboards in React with line, bar, and area charts using Recharts' composable component API without custom D3 code
Create responsive chart components that automatically resize to container dimensions using ResponsiveContainer wrapper
Implement custom chart tooltips and legends using Recharts' render prop API for branded data visualizations
Display agent-generated analytics data (time series, distributions, comparisons) using appropriate chart types with minimal code
Create composed charts with multiple chart types (bar + line overlay) for complex data relationships using ComposedChart
not_for: Complex custom visualizations requiring D3-level control — use D3.js directly or Victory/Nivo for more control
Very large datasets (100K+ data points) — SVG rendering becomes slow; use WebGL-based libraries like Deck.gl for large datasets
best_when: You need common chart types (line, bar, area, pie) in React with a clean component API and don't need custom D3 visualizations.
avoid_when: You need complex custom visualizations, very large datasets, or non-React environments — use D3.js, Deck.gl, or ECharts instead.

pricing.notes: Recharts is MIT open source. Free for personal and commercial use.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — local library. SVG rendering degrades with 10K+ data points.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Silent failures are common in Recharts — missing data or incorrect format causes charts to render empty without helpful error messages. Development experience improved in 2.x but still limited debug output.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Chart rendering is deterministic for same data and configuration.
known_agent_gotchas: Recharts data format requires an array of objects with named keys matching the dataKey prop — incorrect data shape renders an empty chart silently
ResponsiveContainer requires a parent with explicit height — wrapping in a div without set height results in a zero-height chart invisible to users
Recharts 2.x has different API from 1.x for some components — particularly CartesianGrid, XAxis tick formatting, and custom shapes; verify API for the installed version
Custom tooltips must handle the active prop correctly — rendering tooltip HTML without checking active prop displays tooltip content when no data point is selected
SVG-based charts don't support HTML in labels or tooltips — custom renderers must use SVG elements, not HTML divs, for content inside chart SVG containers
Date/time axes require custom tick formatters — Recharts doesn't auto-format dates; implement tickFormatter prop with date-fns or Luxon to display human-readable time labels

security_score_components.security_notes: Local library — no network access. SVG rendering — protect against XSS in custom label renderers that render user data. MIT licensed.
